children.heading: Drugs or devices intended for human or animal use which require approval or licensing
children.label: a Drugs or devices intended for human or animal use which require approval or licensing
i
children.text: requires approval by the Secretary under before such drug may be introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce; orsection 355 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: requires licensing by the Secretary under or by the Secretary of Agriculture under the Act of [ et seq.] (known as the Virus-Serum Toxin Act) before it may be introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce;section 262 of title 4221 U.S.C. 151March 4, 1913

children.label: B
children.text: does not have such approval or license; and

children.label: C
children.text: is not exempt from such sections or Act; and

children.label: A
children.text: does not comply with an applicable requirement under section 360d or 360e of this title;

children.label: B
children.text: under is exempt from either such section; orsection 360j(g) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: is a banned device under , is adulterated, misbranded, and in violation of such sections or Act unless the export of the drug or device is, except as provided in subsection (f), authorized under subsection (b), (c), (d), or (e) or . If a drug or device described in paragraphs (1) and (2) may be exported under subsection (b) and if an application for such drug or device under section 355 or 360e of this title or was disapproved, the Secretary shall notify the appropriate public health official of the country to which such drug will be exported of such disapproval.section 360f of this titlesection 381(e)(2) of this titlesection 262 of title 42

children.heading: List of eligible countries for export; criteria for addition to list; direct export; petition for exemption
children.label: b List of eligible countries for export; criteria for addition to list; direct export; petition for exemption
i
children.text: in Australia, Canada, Israel, Japan, New Zealand, Switzerland, or South Africa; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the European Union or a country in the European Economic Area (the countries in the European Union and the European Free Trade Association) if the drug or device is marketed in that country or the drug or device is authorized for general marketing in the European Economic Area.

children.label: i
children.text: Statutory or regulatory requirements which require the review of drugs and devices for safety and effectiveness by an entity of the government of such country and which authorize the approval of only those drugs and devices which have been determined to be safe and effective by experts employed by or acting on behalf of such entity and qualified by scientific training and experience to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of drugs and devices on the basis of adequate and well-controlled investigations, including clinical investigations, conducted by experts qualified by scientific training and experience to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of drugs and devices.

children.label: I
children.text: the manufacture, processing, and packing of drugs in the country are adequate to preserve their identity, quality, purity, and strength; and

children.label: II
children.text: the manufacture, preproduction design validation, packing, storage, and installation of a device are adequate to assure that the device will be safe and effective.

children.label: iii
children.text: Statutory or regulatory requirements for the reporting of adverse reactions to drugs and devices and procedures to withdraw approval and remove drugs and devices found not to be safe or effective.

children.label: iv
children.text: Statutory or regulatory requirements that the labeling and promotion of drugs and devices must be in accordance with the approval of the drug or device.

children.label: v
children.text: The valid marketing authorization system in such country or countries is equivalent to the systems in the countries described in clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (A).

children.label: C
children.text: An appropriate country official, manufacturer, or exporter may request the Secretary to take action under subparagraph (B) to designate an additional country or countries to be added to the list of countries described in clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (A) by submitting documentation to the Secretary in support of such designation. Any person other than a country requesting such designation shall include, along with the request, a letter from the country indicating the desire of such country to be designated.

children.label: A
children.text: the drug complies with the laws of that country and has valid marketing authorization by the responsible authority in that country; and

children.label: i
children.text: Statutory or regulatory requirements which require the review of drugs for safety and effectiveness by an entity of the government of such country and which authorize the approval of only those drugs which have been determined to be safe and effective by experts employed by or acting on behalf of such entity and qualified by scientific training and experience to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of drugs on the basis of adequate and well-controlled investigations, including clinical investigations, conducted by experts qualified by scientific training and experience to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of drugs.

children.label: ii
children.text: Statutory or regulatory requirements that the methods used in, and the facilities and controls used for the manufacture, processing, and packing of drugs in the country are adequate to preserve their identity, quality, purity, and strength.

children.label: iii
children.text: Statutory or regulatory requirements for the reporting of adverse reactions to drugs and procedures to withdraw approval and remove drugs found not to be safe or effective.

children.label: iv
children.text: Statutory or regulatory requirements that the labeling and promotion of drugs must be in accordance with the approval of the drug.

children.label: i
children.text: certifies that the drug would not meet the conditions for approval under this chapter or the conditions for approval of a country described in clause (i) or (ii) of paragraph (1)(A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: provides the Secretary with credible scientific evidence, acceptable to the Secretary, that the drug would be safe and effective under the conditions of use in the country to which it is being exported; and

children.label: i
children.text: requests approval of the export of the drug to such country;

children.label: ii
children.text: certifies that the health authority understands that the drug is not approved under this chapter or in a country described in clause (i) or (ii) of paragraph (1)(A); and

children.label: iii
children.text: concurs that the scientific evidence provided pursuant to subparagraph (A) is credible scientific evidence that the drug would be reasonably safe and effective in such country.

children.heading: Investigational use exemption
children.label: c Investigational use exemption
children.text: A drug or device intended for investigational use in any country described in clause (i) or (ii) of subsection (b)(1)(A) may be exported in accordance with the laws of that country and shall be exempt from regulation under section 355(i) or 360j(g) of this title.

children.heading: Anticipation of market authorization
children.label: d Anticipation of market authorization
children.text: A drug or device intended for formulation, filling, packaging, labeling, or further processing in anticipation of market authorization in any country described in clause (i) or (ii) of subsection (b)(1)(A) may be exported for use in accordance with the laws of that country.

children.heading: Diagnosis, prevention, or treatment of tropical disease
children.label: e Diagnosis, prevention, or treatment of tropical disease

children.text: A drug or device which is used in the diagnosis, prevention, or treatment of a tropical disease or another disease not of significant prevalence in the United States and which does not otherwise qualify for export under this section shall, upon approval of an application, be permitted to be exported if the Secretary finds that the drug or device will not expose patients in such country to an unreasonable risk of illness or injury and the probable benefit to health from the use of the drug or device (under conditions of use prescribed, recommended, or suggested in the labeling or proposed labeling of the drug or device) outweighs the risk of injury or illness from its use, taking into account the probable risks and benefits of currently available drug or device treatment.

children.label: A
children.text: the receipt of any credible information indicating that the drug or device is being or may have been exported from a country for which the Secretary made a finding under paragraph (1)(A) to a country for which the Secretary cannot make such a finding; and

children.label: B
children.text: the receipt of any information indicating adverse reactions to such drug.

children.label: i
children.text: a drug or device for which an application is approved under paragraph (1) does not continue to meet the requirements of such paragraph; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the holder of an approved application under paragraph (1) has not made the report required by paragraph (2),

children.label: B
children.text: If the Secretary determines that the holder of an approved application under paragraph (1) or an importer is exporting a drug or device from the United States to an importer and such importer is exporting the drug or device to a country for which the Secretary cannot make a finding under paragraph (1) and such export presents an imminent hazard, the Secretary shall immediately prohibit the export of the drug or device to such importer, provide the person exporting the drug or device from the United States prompt notice of the prohibition, and afford such person an opportunity for an expedited hearing.

children.heading: Prohibition of export of drug or device
children.label: f Prohibition of export of drug or device

children.text: if the drug or device is not manufactured, processed, packaged, and held in substantial conformity with current good manufacturing practice requirements or does not meet international standards as certified by an international standards organization recognized by the Secretary;
if the drug or device is adulterated under clause (1), (2)(A), or (3) of section 351(a) or subsection (c) or (d) of ;section 351 of this title
if the requirements of subparagraphs (A) through (D) of have not been met;section 381(e)(1) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: if the drug or device is the subject of a notice by the Secretary or the Secretary of Agriculture of a determination that the probability of reimportation of the exported drug or device would present an imminent hazard to the public health and safety of the United States and the only means of limiting the hazard is to prohibit the export of the drug or device; or

children.label: B
children.text: if the drug or device presents an imminent hazard to the public health of the country to which the drug or device would be exported;

children.label: i
children.text: the country in which the drug or device received valid marketing authorization under subsection (b); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the country to which the drug or device would be exported; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the language and units of measurement of the country to which the drug or device would be exported or in the language designated by such country; or
if the drug or device is not promoted in accordance with the labeling requirements set forth in paragraph (5).

children.heading: Notification of Secretary
children.label: g Notification of Secretary
children.text: The exporter of a drug or device exported under subsection (b)(1) shall provide a simple notification to the Secretary identifying the drug or device when the exporter first begins to export such drug or device to any country listed in clause (i) or (ii) of subsection (b)(1)(A). When an exporter of a drug or device first begins to export a drug or device to a country which is not listed in clause (i) or (ii) of subsection (b)(1)A), the exporter shall provide a simple notification to the Secretary identifying the drug or device and the country to which such drug or device is being exported. Any exporter of a drug or device shall maintain records of all drugs or devices exported and the countries to which they were exported.1So in original. Probably should be subsection “(b)(1)(A),”.1

children.heading: References to Secretary and term “drug”
children.label: h References to Secretary and term “drug”

children.text: a reference to the Secretary shall in the case of a biological product which is required to be licensed under the Act of [ et seq.] () (commonly known as the Virus-Serum Toxin Act) be considered to be a reference to the Secretary of Agriculture, andMarch 4, 191321 U.S.C. 15137 Stat. 832–833
the term “drug” includes drugs for human use as well as biologicals under or the Act of () (commonly known as the Virus-Serum Toxin Act).section 262 of title 4237 Stat. 832–833March 4, 1913

children.heading: Exportation
children.label: i Exportation
children.text: Insulin and antibiotic drugs may be exported without regard to the requirements in this section if the insulin and antibiotic drugs meet the requirements of .section 381(e)(1) of this title
